breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Group Wipes 200,000 Stryker Devices in Global Attack
breaking_news.summary:
The Iran-linked hacktivist group Handala, also known as Void Manticore, claimed responsibility for a massive wiper malware attack against medical technology giant Stryker. The group alleges it erased data from over 200,000 systems, servers, and mobile devices, forcing the company to shut down offices across 79 countries.

This incident represents a significant disruption to critical healthcare infrastructure, with Stryker confirming a global network outage affecting its Microsoft environment. The attack highlights the escalating cyber warfare risks posed by state-sponsored actors targeting essential medical supply chains during geopolitical conflicts.

breaking_news.title: Google Patches Two Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Days in Emergency Update
breaking_news.summary:
Google released an emergency security update for Chrome to address two high-severity zero-day vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-3909 and CVE-2026-3910, which are currently being exploited in the wild. The flaws reside in the Skia graphics library and the V8 JavaScript engine, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted HTML pages.

CISA has added these vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, urging immediate patching for all Chrome users. The active exploitation of these browser flaws poses a severe risk to enterprise and consumer devices, emphasizing the need for rapid patch management strategies.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds Critical n8n Remote Code Execution Flaw to KEV Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a critical remote code execution vulnerability in the n8n workflow automation platform to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-68613, allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code and has been actively exploited in the wild.

With over 24,700 instances potentially exposed, this vulnerability threatens organizations relying on n8n for automation and data integration. CISA has issued a directive requiring federal agencies to patch the flaw immediately to prevent unauthorized server takeovers and credential theft.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 Campaign Uses SEO Poisoning to Distribute Fake VPN Clients
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft Defender Experts uncovered a sophisticated credential theft campaign by the Storm-2561 threat actor, which uses SEO poisoning to lure victims to spoofed enterprise VPN sites. The attackers distribute trojanized installers impersonating legitimate software from vendors like Cisco, Fortinet, and Ivanti to harvest corporate login credentials.

This campaign demonstrates the increasing use of search engine manipulation to bypass security controls and target enterprise users. The stolen credentials can grant attackers access to internal networks, making this a high-priority threat for organizations relying on remote access solutions.

breaking_news.title: Authorities Dismantle SocksEscort Proxy Network Infecting 360,000 Devices
breaking_news.summary:
Law enforcement agencies in the US and Europe disrupted the SocksEscort proxy service, which utilized the AVrecon botnet to compromise approximately 360,000 residential routers and IoT devices. The operation, codenamed Operation Lightning, seized 23 servers and froze $3.5 million in cryptocurrency linked to the criminal infrastructure.

This takedown significantly degrades the capabilities of cybercriminals who rely on residential proxies to mask their activities during fraud and malware campaigns. The disruption of this large-scale botnet highlights the growing threat of IoT device abuse in supporting global cybercrime operations.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters Claims 1 Petabyte Data Theft from Telus Digital
breaking_news.summary:
The extortion group ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen up to 1 petabyte of data from Telus Digital, including support recordings, code, and employee records. The breach involved unauthorized access to the company's systems, with the group leveraging Salesforce and other SaaS platforms to exfiltrate sensitive information.

Telus Digital confirmed the incident and is investigating the scope of the unauthorized access while maintaining business operations. The massive volume of stolen data underscores the risks associated with cloud-based business process outsourcing and the persistence of data theft gangs.

breaking_news.title: Veeam Patches Critical Remote Code Execution Flaws in Backup Software
breaking_news.summary:
Veeam Software released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup & Replication platform, including three flaws with a CVSS score of 9.9. These vulnerabilities allow authenticated users to execute remote code on backup servers, potentially enabling attackers to compromise data recovery capabilities.

Backup systems are high-value targets for ransomware operators, making these flaws particularly dangerous for enterprise data security. Organizations are urged to patch immediately to prevent attackers from destroying backups and escalating their access within the network.

breaking_news.title: Critical HPE Aruba AOS-CX Vulnerability Allows Unauthenticated Admin Reset
breaking_news.summary:
Hewlett Packard Enterprise patched a critical vulnerability in Aruba AOS-CX switches that allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication controls. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-23813, enables remote actors to reset administrator passwords and seize control of enterprise network switches without credentials.

This vulnerability poses a severe risk to network infrastructure security, as compromised switches can facilitate lateral movement and network disruption. HPE recommends isolating management interfaces and applying patches immediately to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access.

breaking_news.title: AppsFlyer Web SDK Hijacked to Spread Crypto-Stealing JavaScript Code
breaking_news.summary:
The AppsFlyer Web SDK was temporarily hijacked in a supply-chain attack that injected malicious JavaScript code designed to steal cryptocurrency. The compromise allowed attackers to distribute crypto-stealing payloads to users of the popular mobile attribution platform.

This incident highlights the vulnerability of software development kits (SDKs) in the mobile ecosystem, where a single compromise can affect numerous downstream applications. Developers are advised to monitor SDK integrity and implement strict dependency checks to prevent similar supply-chain attacks.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm Supply-Chain Attack Abuses 72 Open VSX Extensions
breaking_news.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers identified a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign abusing 72 extensions in the Open VSX registry to target developers. The threat actor utilizes extensionPack and extensionDependencies to propagate malware through transitive dependencies, escalating the sophistication of the supply-chain attack.

This campaign targets the developer ecosystem, aiming to compromise development environments and steal intellectual property or credentials. The use of legitimate registry mechanisms makes detection difficult, requiring enhanced scrutiny of extension dependencies in development workflows.
